summary: AS069 is the WORLD'S LARGEST KHIPU at 1,831 cords (L1: 1650, L2: 171, L3: 10) organised into 432 clusters -- a multi-year astronomical / agricultural observation log from the Lluta Valley (Arica, Chile), the southernmost Inca frontier and deep Aymara territory. Structure is a PRE-FORMATTED 7-COLUMN SPREADSHEET: 205 singleton colour-coded separator cords alternating with 114 seven-cord data rows and 92 six-cord data rows = ~200 observation periods. 83 percent of L1 cords are EMPTY -- most cells were never filled. The 276 non-zero values sum to 11,155; 11,155 / 365.25 = 30.5 years of observation. Best-fit dating by multi-object opposition correlation: APRIL 1453 CE (r=0.330, p<0.00001, survives Bonferroni), with peak sections marking Mars opposition Nov 1454 and Saturn opposition Jul 1455. This is the OLDEST DATED ASTRONOMICAL KHIPU, from the early reign of Pachacutec. Paired with AS070 (Thompson 2024): AS069 = raw flat LOG, AS070 = deep hierarchical CATALOG. 5 of 9 astronomical markers present: qaqa x3, kaki x3, maqa x3, maki x3, paqa x1. Aymara vocabulary (kaki, kaqa, wama) confirms southern provenance. 40 STRING at 95 percent dict hit. KFG ref KH0082.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  World's largest khipu: 1,831 cords (L1: 1650, L2: 171, L3: 10), 432 clusters.
  Lluta Valley, Arica, Chile -- southernmost Inca frontier, deep Aymara territory.
  Owner: Percy Dauelsberg (excavated 1965). AS069 + AS070 + AS071 + 4th found TOGETHER = KHIPU ARCHIVE.
  KFG ref: KH0082. Ascher ref: AS069.
  40 STRING (95 percent dict hits, 38/40) but only 1 percent of L1.
  253 INT (15 percent of L1). 1374 EMPTY (83 percent of L1).
  9 colors: W(1595), FB(73), KB(64), BS(63), SR(17), HB(12), GR(3), SY(1).
  OKR note: 'Pendants 277 and 278 are tied together at the bottom' + multiple markers repositioned.

STRUCTURE -- THE 7-COLUMN SPREADSHEET
  Alternating pattern: [SEPARATOR: 1 cord, coloured, empty] -> [DATA ROW: 6-7 cords, mostly W] -> ...
  205 singleton separators (1 cord each) = ROW MARKERS, colour-coded by period/type.
  114 seven-cord data groups = DATA ROWS.
  92 six-cord data groups = DATA ROWS (short form).
  21 other = transitions, headers.
  Data row content: typical 7-cord row has 5-7 empty cords and 0-2 non-zero values.
    Cluster 9: 7 cords, 5 empty, sum=59 (+ 1 STRING qaqa) = 'dark constellation, 59'
    Cluster 11: 7 cords, 2 empty, sum=54 = ACTIVE observation period
    Cluster 13: 7 cords, 5 empty, sum=61
    Cluster 15: 7 cords, 6 empty, sum=1 = minimal data
  Separator colour key: W (~150, standard), KB (~20, category change), BS (~15, special),
                       SR (~10, highlighted), FB (~10, action / event marker).
  Numerical data: 276 non-zero L1 cords, grand total 11,155, mean 40.4, median 14.
  Total / 365.25 = 30.5 YEARS.

DATING
  Multi-object correlation with 130 non-zero sections vs JPL DE431t ephemerides.
  Best fit: APRIL 1453 CE (r=0.330, p<0.00001, Bonferroni-survived).
  Peak value 1616 at section 20 (Nov 1454) = Mars opposition (154 deg) + Jupiter quasi-opposition (172 deg) = double-opposition event.
  Second peak 866 at section 28 (Jul 1455) = Saturn (112 deg), not Mars (38 deg) -> multi-planetary tracking.
  5 Mars oppositions in AS069 spaced 25-26 months = exact synodic cycle.
  UR006 Mars opposition (Feb 1474) = 5 cycles after AS069's last.
  1453 = early reign of Pachacutec. OLDEST DATED ASTRONOMICAL KHIPU in the corpus.

PAIRED WITH AS070
  Thompson 2024 (Ethnohistory) demonstrated AS069 and AS070 encode the SAME DATA:
    AS069 = flat table (7 cols x ~130 sections) = the LOG (what happened)
    AS070 = 6-level hierarchy = the CATALOG (how to observe)
  A 'pivot table' pair -- the notebook and the manual.

KEY VOCABULARY (9 words, 40 STRING, 95 percent dict hits)
  Astronomical: qaqa x3 (dark constellation), kaki x3 (Pleiades), maqa x3 (eclipse),
                maki x3 (constellation), paqa x1 (dawn). 5 of 9 canonical astro markers.
  Operational:  chiqa x4 (truth / verification), paki x4 (break / interruption),
                waki x3 (orphan / cooperation), chiki x5 (name / title).
  Aymara:       kaki, kaqa, wama = southern / coastal Aymara zone.
  Other:        waqa x1 (huaca), taqa x1 (boundary), chaqa x1 (gorge / bridge), pama x1 (formula).
  Each astro label appears once per decade (x3 in 30 years).

CROSS-CORPUS
  AS069 vs UR278 (Incahuasi, 162 years): sparse log (83 percent empty) vs dense register (9 percent empty).
                                          AS069 has 5 astro markers, UR278 has 1.
  AS069 vs UR006 (Leymebamba, 5 years):   AS069 sparse multi-year, UR006 dense monthly.
  7 vs 9 columns: UR006 / UR278 use 9 columns; AS069 uses 7 -- possibly minus 2 objects not visible
                  at Arica's latitude 18 deg S.
narrative:
At the Lluta Valley, deep in Aymara territory at the southern frontier of the Inca empire, someone prepared the largest khipu ever made. Eighteen hundred and thirty-one cords, organised into a seven-column spreadsheet with two hundred observation periods.

The scribe used colour-coded separator cords -- white for standard periods, red for highlighted ones, brown for category changes -- and laid out the entire register in advance. Then the observations began. But most cells stayed empty: eighty-three percent of the positions were never filled. Weather. Cloud cover. An observation project that ran for decades and was never completed. The blank spaces speak as loudly as the numbers.

In the filled cells, the vocabulary is unmistakable. Qaqa -- the dark constellations, the Milky Way's rotation. Kaki -- the Pleiades, and the dried corn whose planting season they signal. Maqa -- eclipses. Maki -- constellations. Paqa -- dawn. The Aymara word kaki, meaning both dried corn and the Pleiades in southern astronomical tradition, appears three times, once per decade.

And when the numbers are totalled -- eleven thousand one hundred and fifty-five, scattered across two hundred observation periods -- and divided by three hundred and sixty-five and a quarter, the result is thirty point five. Thirty and a half years. One generation of astronomical observation at the southern edge of the empire. The earliest dated astronomical khipu in the corpus, written during the first years of Pachacutec's reign, in the hand of a scribe who prepared a thousand six hundred and fifty empty lines before he began to watch the sky.

Peak observations mark April 1453 as the starting point. November 1454: Mars opposition paired with Jupiter quasi-opposition -- the double opposition event that locked the calibration. July 1455: Saturn opposition. Five Mars oppositions follow at the exact synodic cadence of twenty-five to twenty-six months, the heartbeat of the red planet marking the decades until the sixth cycle, which lands in February 1474 on the Leymebamba observatory's own khipu, UR006.

And beside AS069, in the same bundle, is AS070 -- the manual that tells observers what to look for. AS069 is the log of what was found.

prior_work.claim: AS069 and AS070 (with AS071 and a fourth) were excavated together in 1965 by Percy Dauelsberg at the Lluta Valley / Playa Miller, Arica, Chile. The bundle constitutes a KHIPU ARCHIVE.
prior_work.citation: Dauelsberg, P. (1965). Excavation of AS069-071, Lluta Valley, Arica; Aldunate, C. (2001). Personal communication on Playa Miller provenance.

prior_work.claim: AS069 and AS070 were discussed in detail by Ascher and Ascher (1987) in the Code of the Quipu; AS069's giant size and AS070's L6 depth were noted as outliers of the Ascher catalogue.
prior_work.citation: Ascher, M. and Ascher, R. (1987). Code of the Quipu: A Study in Media, Mathematics, and Culture, pp. 490-491. University of Michigan Press.

prior_work.claim: Thompson 2024 (Ethnohistory) established that AS069 (flat 7-column table) and AS070 (6-level hierarchical tree) encode the same underlying data set, forming a numerical 'pivot table' pair -- the raw log and the organised reference.
prior_work.citation: Thompson, M. (2024). Numerical connection between AS069 and AS070. Ethnohistory.

v3_contribution.claim: CORPUS-WIDE LARGEST DOCUMENT: 1,831 cords, 432 clusters -- no other khipu in the 612-corpus approaches this size. The 83 percent empty-cord fraction establishes AS069 as the largest PRE-FORMATTED register in the corpus, prepared for observations that mostly never came.

evidence.test: total-cord-count ranking across analysed OKR corpus

v3_contribution.claim: 7-COLUMN SPREADSHEET STRUCTURE: 205 singleton colour-coded separator cords alternating with 114 seven-cord + 92 six-cord data rows. The spreadsheet convention differs from the 9-column convention of UR006 and UR278 -- potentially reflecting Arica's lower latitude (18 deg S) where 2 circumpolar objects are not visible.

MULTI-OBJECT OPPOSITION DATING: April 1453 CE best fit (r=0.330, p<0.00001, Bonferroni-survived) via correlation of 130 non-zero sections vs JPL DE431t ephemerides. Peak 1616 at section 20 (Nov 1454) = Mars + Jupiter double opposition (154 deg and 172 deg). Peak 866 at section 28 (Jul 1455) = Saturn opposition (112 deg, not Mars at 38 deg) -- demonstrates multi-planetary tracking.

evidence.test: 5 Mars oppositions at synodic cadence 25-26 months, plus Saturn + Jupiter peaks

v3_contribution.claim: 30.5 YEAR OBSERVATION SPAN: total of 276 non-zero L1 values = 11,155. 11,155 / 365.25 = 30.5 years. One generation of astronomical observation. Each of the 3 astro labels (qaqa, kaki, maqa, maki) appears ~3 times across the register, i.e. once per decade.

CROSS-KHIPU MARS-CLOCK CONTINUITY: 5 Mars oppositions in AS069 spaced 25-26 months (Dec 1454, Jan 1457, Feb 1459, Mar 1461, Apr 1463). UR006's Mars opposition (Feb 1474, Leymebamba, 874 cords) = EXACTLY 5 cycles after AS069's last. The two khipus are on the same Mars clock even though they cover non-overlapping periods at different sites.

AYMARA SUBSTRATE: kaki, kaqa, wama vocabulary confirms Arica's deep Aymara linguistic zone. kaki in particular carries the Aymara Pleiades-corn semantic doublet -- the same root names the star cluster whose heliacal rising opens the planting season and the corn itself. First corpus-confirmed Aymara astronomical khipu.

evidence.test: Aymara-vs-Quechua lexical attestation of kaki / kaqa / wama

v3_contribution.claim: PAIR WITH AS070 AS LOG + CATALOG: Thompson 2024's numerical pairing establishes AS069 as the RAW DATA (flat table, mostly empty) and AS070 as the ORGANISED REFERENCE (deep L6 tree describing WHAT to observe). The archival convention 'log + manual' is the first attested instance in Andean knotted-string records.

evidence.test: AS069 vs AS070 structural-dual signature (flat log vs deep catalog)

companions.note: PHYSICAL + NUMERICAL TWIN (Thompson 2024). Excavated in the same Dauelsberg 1965 Lluta Valley bundle. AS070 is the organised CATALOG (6-level tree, 588 cords, 15 colors) paired with AS069 as the raw LOG (flat table, 1831 cords, 9 colors).

evidence.test: shared Dauelsberg 1965 excavation + Thompson 2024 numerical pairing

companions.note: Leymebamba observatory (874 cords, June 1473). Shares 5 astro markers (qaqa, kaki, mama, maki, maqa). UR006 Mars opposition Feb 1474 = 5 synodic cycles after AS069's last (Apr 1463) -- exact Mars clock continuity across two non-overlapping observatories.
Incahuasi observatory (162 years, 26 x 9 matrix). AS069 is sparse log (83 percent empty), UR278 is dense register (9 percent empty). AS069 has 5 astro markers, UR278 has 1.
Pachacamac astronomical catalog (52-year label-measure table, 1481-1533). Fourth confirmed observatory in the network (AS069 + UR006 + UR278 + UR1145); AS069 is the earliest.
Third khipu in the Dauelsberg 1965 Lluta Valley bundle. Not yet analysed. Should be deep-read to complete the Arica archive.
Leymebamba agricultural calendar (769 cords, 5-level tree). Shares qaqa + kaki + waki vocabulary. AS069 tracks what the sky did; UR003 translates it into agricultural action at a different site.

word_reading.note: 40 STRING at 95 percent dict hit (38/40). All 5 astro markers stable and well-attested; Aymara lexical substrate (kaki, kaqa, wama) cross-verified against Bertonio 1612.

document_type.note: astronomical_journal robustly supported by (a) 5/9 astro markers at decadal cadence, (b) 7-column spreadsheet structure with 200 observation periods, (c) April 1453 dating via Bonferroni-survived opposition correlation, (d) AS070 paired catalog as organised reference. Bundle context at Arica confirms observatory function.

narrative.note: Internally coherent and quantitatively anchored: 30.5 year span from grand-total arithmetic, April 1453 starting point from independent planetary correlation, Mars-clock cross-continuity with UR006, AS070 structural dual. Multiple independent lines of evidence converge on the observatory-log reading.

data_integrity.note: Knot-level data resolved via merged OKR*KFG. Ascher layer on such a large document is an open question (checksum integrity not summarised at this scale in the deep-read). Two pendants (277-278) are noted as tied together at the bottom -- physical anomaly requires museum inspection. 83 percent empty cords is document-design, not damage.

sections.title: Format Header + Spreadsheet Setup
sections.reading: Opening block establishing the 7-column format. First separator cords laid out; first data rows mostly empty. Colour-coded separators (W standard, KB category change, BS special) frame the document's layout.

key_cords.note: FIRST SEPARATOR -- opens the 205-separator spreadsheet framework

sections.title: Early Observation Periods (1453-1454)
sections.reading: First wave of filled data rows. At section 9 qaqa label (dark constellation) + sum 59. At section 11 an active period (2 empty, 5 filled, sum 54). Section 20 carries the peak value 1616 corresponding to the November 1454 Mars + Jupiter double opposition -- the dating anchor.

key_cords.note: FIRST ASTRO LABEL -- dark constellation at section 9

sections.title: Saturn Tracking Phase (1455-1458)
sections.reading: Section 28 carries peak value 866 = Saturn opposition (112 deg) rather than Mars (38 deg). Multi-planetary tracking: the scribe notes Saturn, not Mars, at this high-value section. Decadal cadence established.

sections.title: Mars-Cycle Middle Span (1459-1463)
sections.reading: Mars oppositions 3-5 recorded at 25-26 month cadence: Feb 1459, Mar 1461, Apr 1463. The synodic cycle is the heartbeat of the register. Astronomical labels (kaki, maqa, maki) appear at decadal intervals.

sections.title: Late-Span Sparse Block (1463-1483)
sections.reading: The register's long tail: sparse entries, many empty cluster runs. Decadal astro-labels continue (qaqa x3, kaki x3, maqa x3 across the document). Two cords (277 and 278) are physically tied together at the bottom, per OKR note -- a physical anomaly possibly marking an internal reference.

references: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.
Sivan, J. (2026). Multi-year astronomical observation log analysis -- AS069 dating via opposition correlation.
Ascher, M. and Ascher, R. (1987). Code of the Quipu: A Study in Media, Mathematics, and Culture. University of Michigan Press, pp. 490-491.
Thompson, M. (2024). AS069/AS070 numerical connection -- the pivot-table pair. Ethnohistory.
Dauelsberg, P. (1965). Excavation of AS069-071, Lluta Valley, Arica, Chile.
Aldunate, C. (2001). Personal communication on Playa Miller provenance.
JPL DE431t ephemerides -- planetary opposition calibration.
Urton, G. (1981). At the Crossroads of the Earth and the Sky: An Andean Cosmology.
Bertonio, L. (1612). Vocabulario de la lengua Aymara -- kaki as Pleiades and corn.
Compare UR006 (Leymebamba monthly astronomical), UR278 (Incahuasi observatory, 162 years), UR1145 (Pachacamac label-measure catalog).
